_doc: CPM60.COM is the as-ASSEMBLED 60K installer. The os/ component sources were recovered from a BOOTED memory image, so they are the RUNTIME forms (self-modified / relocated / sysgen-filled). build_cpm60_com() places those sources, then applies these as-shipped values to undo the runtime mutations. Every byte below is one known install/boot operation -- nothing is unexplained.

groups.name: boot_lc_handoff_glue
groups.why: The boot loader's $1000 page AS SHIPPED: LDA $C081 x2 (Language-Card read+write enable), TXA / LSR x4 / TAY / PHA / STA $C088,X (slot-index setup). At boot the relocator overwrites $1000-$100D with its own entry and the planted Z-80 reset stub 'JP $FA00' (C3 00 FA); os/CPM_BootLoader.s captured that post-run state.

groups.name: boot_self_patches
groups.why: Two boot-loader bytes the loader rewrites in place at runtime ($1069 C0->C1, $1176 98->4C).

groups.name: installfragments_unrelocated
groups.why: The COM ships the UNRELOCATED InstallFragments template: a STA $FFFF placeholder at $03C7 (boot relocator rewrites to STA $C700) plus zeroed address/parameter cells the relocator fills. os/CPM_InstallFragments.s holds the relocated form.

groups.name: ccp_sysgen_pointers
groups.why: Four CCP-internal pointer words at $DB5C-$DB63 ($D393,$D3FC,$D447,$D6?? in the booted image) that ship as ZERO and are filled at sysgen/relocation. The shared CCP source builds the booted (filled) form, so the COM's zeros are restored here.
